prompt: The repository contains parser.py and report.py. Fix the CSV parsing/reporting behavior so all hidden tests pass. Requirements: parse_orders(csv_text) must handle quoted commas via the standard csv module, ignore blank lines, convert amount to float, reject rows missing order_id/customer/amount/status, and preserve status case as given. summarize_by_customer(rows) must return a dict keyed by customer with total_amount and order_count, excluding rows whose status is exactly 'cancelled'. Do not change function names. Return complete corrected contents of every changed file using <<<FILE path>>> blocks and nothing else.

inputs.name: parser.py
inputs.content:
def parse_orders(csv_text):
    lines = csv_text.strip().split('\n')
    header = lines[0].split(',')
    rows = []
    for line in lines[1:]:
        parts = line.split(',')
        rows.append(dict(zip(header, parts)))
    return rows

inputs.name: report.py

files.content:
def parse_orders(csv_text):
    lines = csv_text.strip().split('\n')
    header = lines[0].split(',')
    rows = []
    for line in lines[1:]:
        parts = line.split(',')
        rows.append(dict(zip(header, parts)))
    return rows

testFiles.content:
from parser import parse_orders
from report import summarize_by_customer

csv_text = '''order_id,customer,amount,status\n1,Acme,10.50,paid\n2,"Beta, Inc",20,cancelled\n\n3,Acme,4.25,paid\n4,"Beta, Inc",7.75,PAID\n'''
rows = parse_orders(csv_text)
assert len(rows) == 4, rows
assert rows[1]['customer'] == 'Beta, Inc', rows[1]
assert rows[0]['amount'] == 10.5 and isinstance(rows[0]['amount'], float)
assert rows[3]['status'] == 'PAID'
summary = summarize_by_customer(rows)
assert summary == {
    'Acme': {'total_amount': 14.75, 'order_count': 2},
    'Beta, Inc': {'total_amount': 7.75, 'order_count': 1},
}, summary
try:
    parse_orders('order_id,customer,amount,status\n5,,9,paid\n')
    raise AssertionError('expected ValueError')
except ValueError:
    pass
print('ok')
